statement.markdown: Solve the problem and fix the bugs.
statement.outputFormat: Return true if the number is prime and false otherwise.
statement.notes: Note that a prime number is a natural number greater than 1 that has no positive divisors other than 1 and itself.

hints.text: Think about the case where you have a number with multiple factors.

editorial.title: Editorial: Correct Primality Test
editorial.intuition: Primality test should return true only if the number has no other factors than 1 and itself.
editorial.bruteForce: A naive approach is to check all numbers from 2 to n-1 to see if they divide n, but this is inefficient.
editorial.optimalSolution: An optimal solution would be to use a more efficient algorithm like the AKS primality test.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The corrected algorithm will only return true when a number has no other factors than 1 and itself.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run of the AKS primality test.
commonMistakes: Not checking for 1 as a factor
Not checking for n as a factor
